content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify validation, malformed, depth, entity, encoding, dangerous-key, oversized, cancellation, and unsupported-format failures with bounded evidence.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Parser Failure Taxonomy model · Theory Feed Ts P…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Parser Failure Taxonomy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Parser Failure Taxonomy model · Theory Feed Ts P…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 5519 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove bounded XML options, entities, depth, dangerous-key rejection, and causal failures; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify validation, malformed, depth, entity, encoding, dangerous-key, oversized, cancellation, and unsupported-format failures with bounded evidence.
